phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your hop-and-slow kiting beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — hop-and-slow kiting edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your hop-and-slow kiting beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — hop-and-slow kiting edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your hop-and-slow kiting on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your hop-and-slow kiting in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your hop-and-slow kiting out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your hop-and-slow kiting beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean his way — the snare-shield poke combos out-tick your hop-and-slow kiting in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 2: Lux's snare-shield poke combos edge out your hop-and-slow kiting on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the trade loop: the knockback-slow into Q-recast is your bread-and-butter chunk.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — three-spell spirit stacks proc your passive haste; trade in full patterns now.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — hop-and-slow kiting edge out the snare-shield poke combos when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.why: Luden's spike: the full Q-E-Q rotation chunks half bars. Force trades on his cooldown gaps.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Aurora warps teamfight space: drop R on the objective clump and hop through the panic.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-poke and recast on his last-hits — the spirit-mark detonation chips harder than the lane expects.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the hop + brief stealth is your trade exit and your gank insurance in one button.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the trade loop: the knockback-slow into Q-recast is your bread-and-butter chunk.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — three-spell spirit stacks proc your passive haste; trade in full patterns now.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Between Worlds online — the zone re-prices every fight: enemies inside feed you speed and exits.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yone's double-blade spirit dives edge out your hop-and-slow kiting on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Aurora warps teamfight space: drop R on the objective clump and hop through the panic.
